channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SpaceX is the top contender to build Trump’s ‘Golden Dome’ missile shield, sources say
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX and two partners have emerged as frontrunners to win a crucial part of President Donald Trump’s “Golden Dome” missile defense shield, six people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps Trailer Arrives - Marvel's MCU Phase 6 Film Set for July Release
item.description: Marvel has released a new trailer for The Fantastic Four: First Steps, and the highly anticipated film is set to arrive in theaters worldwide this July. Get ready for an unmissable two-and-a-half-minute feast for the eyes!
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Best practices for migrating between public clouds
item.description: Historically, cloud migration usually meant moving on-premises workloads to a public cloud, like Amazon Web Services (AWS) or Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kyndryl launches private cloud services for enterprise AI deployments
item.description: Kyndryl has taken the wraps off a suite of private cloud services designed for enterprise customers that want to rapidly deploy AI applications in production environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The next Marvel Rivals patch is adding costume color customization, but players will need to spend real money to get the drip they want
item.description: Marvel Rivals is getting costume color customization in the next update, but it will cost players real money.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Major TalkTalk Business outage means customers have had to go without email for over a week
item.description: Some TalkTalk Business customers had email issues for around a week, with many unhappy with customer service.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia expects ban on chip exports to China to cost $5.5B
item.description: Nvidia now expects new US government restrictions on exports of its H20 chip to China will cost the company as much as $5.5 billion, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This smart Reddit answer engine puts Google Search to shame
item.description: If you follow much tech news, you’ve probably read about the Reddit theory of search. The Reddit theory is the idea that the best info you can get from Googling anything these days comes from Reddit—and the power of crowdsourced wisdom.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: The cybersecurity law that quietly underpins U.S. digital defenses is about to expire
item.description: Nearly a decade after Congress passed the Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015, the law is facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will let players repair their gear, but only if they download an app
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will give players the ability to repair their equipment with a companion app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: South Korean full-stack AI startup Upstage eyes expansion in the US and Japan
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is ramping up global expansion in the US and Japan, positioning its full-stack AI platform to serve enterprise clients.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Another California city bans ‘price-fixing’ software from RealPage to set rents
item.description: San Diego joins several other cities that have voted to ban RealPage software, saying it illegally boosts residential rent prices.
item.dc:creator: Phillip Molnar, The Mercury News

item.title: A handy new Android memory superpower
item.description: Tell me if this sounds familiar: You stumble onto something scintillating during your various digital dalliances — something you think might be a useful nugget of knowledge for work or maybe even a pertinent update for your personal life.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI wants to be more like Apple and Google
item.description: Could it be any clearer that Sam Altman intends for OpenAI to be a sprawling consumer tech company, not just an AI lab? His public comments certainly suggest as much.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cohere’s Embed 4 model helps enterprises search dynamic documents, ‘messy’ data
item.description: Embedding models help transform complex data — text, images, audio, and video — into numerical representations that computers can understand. The embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the data, making them useful for tasks like search, recommendation systems, and natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FTC grills Zuckerberg over emails from Instagram, WhatsApp acquisitions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) grilled Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday over dozens of emails and messages he exchanged with other executives in the lead-up to the social media giant’s acquisitions of Instagram and WhatsApp.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Legacy broadcasters account for just more than 10 percent of TV viewership in March
item.description: Legacy media companies amassed only 10 percent of total television viewership during the month of March, according to new figures from Nielsen Media Research. Nielsen's monthly TV viewership report noted Disney, Paramount, Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Hello, Flipper! Google has developed a genAI model to talk to dolphins
item.description: Google has unveiled a new generative AI (genAI) model, Dolphin Gemma, which will attempt to decode how dolphins communicate with each other. Dolphins are among the most intelligent animals on the planet and communicate partly through whistles and calls.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DDoS-protection crisis looms as attacks grow
item.description: Every year, distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks break records for frequency, size, and sophistication, making it imperative that enterprises adopt powerful mitigation measures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 10 security flaws leave millions vulnerable
item.description: Microsoft patches 12 critical flaws, but six have already been exploited by criminals. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says updating your system is only a short-term fix.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smarter dairy farms where robots milk the cows
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how robots can milk, feed and clean cows on dairy farms, boosting efficiency and comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: CrowdStrike’s field tech strategist explains how AWS Control Tower plus CrowdStrike Falcon lets new workloads inherit access controls in zero trust environments The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: CrowdStrike’s Dennen Monks on ensuring none of your assets escape zero trust coverage first
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World
